response:
Looking at each option:

A) Liquid water holds its shape - Incorrect. Liquids do NOT hold their shape; they take the shape of their container. Only solids maintain their own shape.

B) Water changes to a gas as it boils - Correct. Boiling is the phase transition where liquid water becomes water vapor (gas) at 100°C.

C) Ice becomes a liquid when it freezes - Incorrect. This reverses the process. Freezing is when liquid becomes solid. Ice (solid) becoming liquid is called **melting**, not freezing.

D) Solid ice changes shape with its container - Incorrect. Solids maintain their own shape and volume; they don't conform to their container.

B
